title: Los Angeles Dodgers vs. New York Yankees: Here's How to Watch the MLB Game Live Online
description:
If you purchase an independently reviewed product or service through a link on our website, Variety may receive an affiliate commission.

	After the
body:
If you purchase an independently reviewed product or service through a link on our website, Variety may receive an affiliate commission.

	After the MLB All-Star Week, the two of the biggest markets go head-to-head to compete for bragging right of the best city in the league. We’d also see which is the better league, the National League or American League.

	On Sunday, July 19, and the second game of a doubleheader, the Los Angeles Dodgers takes on the New York Yankees at Yankee Stadium in The Bronx, New York.

	With L.A. competing against New York, it’s likely the game may features a slew of celebrities in attendance to support their favorite teams, such as Dodgers-fans Rob Lowe, Will Ferrell, Hailey Bieber and others, and Yankees-fans Spike Lee, Jay-Z, Jennifer Lopez and more.


	Meanwhile, it’s a prime time game on a major broadcast network, so it’s likely that it’s going to be a big ratings draw.
	
		When Is Los Angeles Dodgers vs. New York Yankees 2026? Date, Time	

	Broadcasting on NBC, first pitch of the game is at 7:20 p.m. ET/4:20 p.m. PT. However, NBC Sports‘ coverage starts at 7 p.m. ET/4 p.m. PT.
	
		How to Watch Los Angeles Dodgers vs. New York Yankees Online	

	The Dodgers-Yankees is available to stream on Sling TV online, while other way sto watch NBC without cable is through web-based cable alternative services, such as DirecTV, Hulu + Live TV, Peacock and others.


						
			
	

					Sling TV
	
					

	Sling is one of the best options for affordable live streaming is with the Sling Orange + Blue package, which goes for $60.99/month afterwards. The package includes NBC, as well as more than 50 live channels, and 50 hours of cloud DVR to record programming. Please note: Pricing and channel availability are subject to your local TV market.



						
			
	

					DirecTV
	
					

	DirecTV offers NBC with their “Entertainment” package and above with prices starting at $59.99 for the first month ($89.99/month afterward), while the service includes more than 90 channels — such as ABC, CBS, FOX, ESPN, FOX Sports, TNT and others. The streamer has a free 5-day trial available, which is more than enough time to watch online for free. You can either cancel or keep the service after the free trial is over.


						
			
	

					NBCUniversal
	
					

	The Dodgers-Yankees livestream is accessible on Peacock, so the best way to watch the game is to purchase a subscription starting at $10.99/month here. Not a subscriber? You can get a Peacock monthly subscription to stream L.A. Dodgers vs. New York Yankees online, and keep it to watch other hits — including “Obsession,” “The Wild Robot,” “How To Train Your Dragon,” “Reminders of Him,” “The Traitors,” “Vanderpump Rules” and others.


						
			
	

					Fubo
	
					

	At $63.99 for the first month of service ($88.99/month afterwards), Fubo is one of the best options for streaming the Dodgers-Yankees game. The service features NBC, as well as more than 220 live TV channels for cable-free streaming and an unlimited cloud DVR. Watch the game with Fubo’s 5-day free trial. You can even stream on up to two devices at the same time.


						
			
	

					Hulu
	
					

	Starting at $89.99/month, Hulu + Live TV is a great option for live channel streaming access to NBC, along with more than 100 other channels, as well as access to hit originals on Disney+, Hulu and ESPN Unlimited.

schema.headline: Los Angeles Dodgers vs. New York Yankees: Here's How to Watch the MLB Game Live Online

title: ‘The Odyssey’ Becomes Christopher Nolan’s Biggest Global Debut of All Time With $264 Million
description:
“The Odyssey” is off to an epic start at the box office.

	Universal’s action-adventure generated $264.1 million globally over the weekend, includin
body:
“The Odyssey” is off to an epic start at the box office.

	Universal’s action-adventure generated $264.1 million globally over the weekend, including $139.6 million internationally and $124.5 million domestically. It’s a towering debut for a movie that’s R-rated, nearly three hours in length and isn’t part of a major franchise. Of course, “The Odyssey” isn’t original — it’s adapted from Homer’s roughly 3,000-year-old epic poem — but the film’s initial success is a testament to Nolan’s outsized popularity as a filmmaker. He’s long been a box office draw, having directed blockbusters like “The Dark Knight” trilogy, “Inception,” “Dunkirk” and “Interstellar.” But Nolan reached new levels of acclaim after 2023’s “Oppenheimer” became a box office behemoth and won the Oscar for best picture.
	

	“The Odyssey” marks the biggest worldwide opening of Nolan’s career, ahead of 2012’s The Dark Knight Rises” with $249 million and 2009’s “The Dark Knight” with $198 million. (This benchmark comes with a caveat because ticket sales weren’t adjusted for inflation.) It’s also the third-biggest opening of the year behind “The Super Mario Galaxy Movie” ($372.5 million) and “Toy Story 5” ($312 million).


	Nolan wrote and directed “The Odyssey,” which stars Matt Damon as Odysseus, the king of Ithaca, who endures a treacherous journey to get home after the Trojan War. Tom Holland, Anne Hathaway, Robert Pattinson, Lupita Nyong’o, Zendaya, Charlize Theron, Elliot Page, Jon Bernthal and John Leguizamo round out the starry ensemble. With stellar reviews and great word-of-mouth, “The Odyssey” is primed for a long, long run in theaters. Universal is backing the $250 million-budgeted movie.
	

	“The Odyssey” opened in 73 international markets, with top territories including the United Kingdom and Ireland with $17.4 million, France with $13.7 million and Italy with $11.5 million. “The Odyssey” stands as Nolan’s biggest opening in 48 of those territories, including France and Italy, as well as Universal’s largest debut in 11 markets. The film has yet to open in South Korea, China or Japan.

	“With ‘The Odyssey,’ we had the extraordinary opportunity to unite moviegoers worldwide around a story that has resonated across cultures for centuries. Christopher Nolan‘s passion, vision and unique cinematic voice consistently drive anticipation and attendance on a truly global scale. He, alongside the force that is Emma Thomas, continues to set the standard for ambitious, event filmmaking,” said Veronika Kwan Vandenberg, president of distribution for Universal Pictures International. “‘The Odyssey’ is that rare kind of movie that brings audiences together across every market in a shared cultural moment.”

	“The Odyssey” is notable as the first feature shot entirely with Imax cameras, and those premium large formats contributed a sizable portion of initial ticket sales. The movie earned $22.2 million internationally (16% of revenues) and $51.8 million globally (23% of revenues) in Imax, delivering the company’s biggest opening in like-for-like markets ahead of “Avengers: Endgame.” And it’s currently pacing 47% ahead of “Oppenheimer,” which was particularly huge in Imax with $35 million worldwide to start and $180 million by the end of its run.

	“If you don’t see ‘The Odyssey’ in Imax, you aren’t seeing it in the way that Chris Nolan intended,” says the company’s CEO Richard Gelfond. He called this weekend a “transcendent moment” for Imax, predicting the ripple effect from “The Odyssey” will lead to “more Imax theaters opening and more audiences seeking out” the format.

schema.headline: ‘The Odyssey’ Becomes Christopher Nolan’s Biggest Global Debut of All Time With $264 Million

title: Ben Shapiro Calls ‘The Odyssey’ a ‘Masterpiece’ Despite Conservative Outrage: Christopher Nolan Is the ‘Best Working Director Today’
description: Ben Shapiro posted an 18-minute review of Christopher Nolan’s “The Odyssey” to his YouTube channel on Saturday, in which he called the Greek epic a
body:
Ben Shapiro posted an 18-minute review of Christopher Nolan’s “The Odyssey” to his YouTube channel on Saturday, in which he called the Greek epic a “masterpiece” despite the conservative outrage about the film’s cast and historical accuracy.

	“Nolan is unparalleled. He’s the best director working today, without a doubt. He just creates iconic movie after iconic movie,” Shapiro said. He also had lots of praise for star Matt Damon, adding, “I think he’s great in nearly everything. He sort of disappears into the part in a way that he doesn’t in a lot of other movies. He’s great as Odysseus.”
	

	He likewise gushed about several other members of the supporting cast: “Robert Pattinson as Antinous is an excellent antagonist. He’s sleazy and gross. Tom Holland as Telemachus is excellent, well cast. Anne Hathaway does a great job as Penelope.”


	Shapiro also fired back at those who complained about Lupita Nyong’o playing Helen of Troy.

	“So, I know a lot of people were like, ‘Oh my god, Lupita Nyong’o is supposed to look like Diane Kruger.’ I don’t know. Diane Krueger doesn’t look very Greek. She’s German,” Shapiro said. “Lupita Nyong’o is a beautiful woman. But here’s the thing, actually, in the script, Nolan goes out of his way to explain that the war wasn’t really launched over Helen being gorgeous.”

	The political commentator did, however, take issue with Elliot Page’s casting as Sinon. He called Page’s role an “absolute puzzler” before pointing out that he is “about 5’2 and 105 pounds soaking wet and has the voice of a woman. It doesn’t work.”
	

	Despite outrage from right-wing voices like Elon Musk, Nolan recently told The Telegraph that he’s unbothered by the noise. He explained that online backlash “comes with the territory” when making a blockbuster.

	“Remember, I spent 10 years of my life dealing with Batman,” Nolan explained. “When I came on ‘Begins,’ writers and artists had been working on this beloved character for almost 65 years, and a lot of freighted thoughts were out there about what he represents. And what I learnt over my time on that trilogy is you can’t worry about any of that at all. What you have to do is honor the original text by interpreting it in the strongest way you personally can.”

schema.headline: Ben Shapiro Calls ‘The Odyssey’ a ‘Masterpiece’ Despite Conservative Outrage: Christopher Nolan Is the ‘Best Working Director Today’

title: Anne Hathaway Says ‘Princess Diaries 3’ Had a ‘Story Breakthrough’ After Having to ‘Start Over’ With the Script: ‘We are Moving in the Right Direction’
description: “Princess Diaries 3” recently had a breakthrough after a scrapped script, Anne Hathaway revealed during an appearance on SiriusXM’s “Radio Andy.”
body:
“Princess Diaries 3” recently had a breakthrough after a scrapped script, Anne Hathaway revealed during an appearance on SiriusXM’s “Radio Andy.” 

	“I’m busy making baby number three, and that has sort of taken the place of knowing when exactly I can make ‘Princess Diaries 3,’” Hathaway said.

	“I can say I think we had a story breakthrough,” she added. “I think we are moving in the right direction. The script that we were working on — we kind of had to start over with this new direction, which is not the update I think anybody wants, but we all feel really good that this is gonna be the one.”
	

	The continuation of “The Princess Diaries” series has been in production since 2022. Adele Lim (“Crazy Rich Asians,” “Raya and the Last Dragon”) signed on to direct in 2024.

	“We just want to make the story right because it is a franchise that is so beloved by so many people,” Lim told Variety at the Gold Gala carpet in May.

	Hathaway will reprise her role as Mia Thermopolis, now Queen of Genovia, in the threequel. Lim teased that fans of the original movies can expect “a lot of fun returns” from other cast members.

	Lim said in May that the shooting and production schedule were still being worked out, and a release date has yet to be announced.

	“The Princess Diaries” was released in 2001 and was followed by the 2004 sequel, “The Princess Diaries 2: Royal Engagement.” The two films have earned a combined $300 million worldwide.

schema.headline: Anne Hathaway Says ‘Princess Diaries 3’ Had a ‘Story Breakthrough’ After Having to ‘Start Over’ With the Script: ‘We are Moving in the Right Direction’

title: ‘Shark Tank’ Star Kevin O’Leary Sued For Claiming Utah Data Center Opposition Has Ties to China
description: Kevin O’Leary is being sued for defamation by a group fighting data center construction in Utah after the “Shark Tank” star went on Fox News and cla
body:
Kevin O’Leary is being sued for defamation by a group fighting data center construction in Utah after the “Shark Tank” star went on Fox News and claimed some of the individuals involved with the opposition have ties to China, according to The Hill.

	The suit, which was also against Fox News, was filed on Wednesday in Utah federal district court by Alliance for a Better Utah, Elevate Strategies, Gabrielle Finlayson and Josh Kanter. It claimed that Kanter and Finlayson “suffered devastating reputational harm, significant economic losses, severe emotional distress, and ongoing threats to their physical safety” after O’Leary went “on the attack” during his Fox News appearance. 
	

	“Fox was instrumental in O’Leary’s defamatory smear campaign, putting him on air to attack Plaintiffs five times in three weeks,” the suit read. “Despite O’Leary’s inherently unreliable claims, and his clear lack of support for them, Fox repeatedly invited O’Leary onto its programs and allowed him to broadcast his false accusations to millions of viewers without any qualification.”


	Fox News Media said in a statement regarding the lawsuit, “Fox News Media publicly corrected the record on every program where on-air guest Kevin O’Leary’s comments were made, all of which was extensively publicized. We will vigorously defend against this lawsuit.”

	Reps for O’Leary did not immediately respond to a request for comment. 

	In late June, Fox News issued a statement apologizing for O’Leary’s claims. The statement read, “Kevin O’Leary appeared as a guest on the show on May 24 and discussed the ongoing controversy surrounding his planned data center project in Utah. He made certain claims relating to the opponents of his project. Mr. O’Leary has now corrected the record and explained he has ‘no evidence’ that the Alliance for a Better Utah, Josh Kantor or Taylor Knuth are funded by China or the Chinese Communist Party.”
	

	O’Leary issued a statement of his own over social media, walking back the allegations. He wrote, “Recently I appeared on various news programs and would like to clarify that I have no evidence that Alliance for a Better Utah, Elevate Strategies, Gabrielle Finlayson, Taylor Knuth or Josh Kanter are funded by China or the Chinese Communist Party.”

schema.headline: ‘Shark Tank’ Star Kevin O’Leary Sued For Claiming Utah Data Center Opposition Has Ties to China

title: HBO’s ‘Harry Potter’ Cinematographer Says It’s ‘Brutal’ Working Around the Young Stars’ Strict Shooting Schedules: ‘Have to Pre-Shoot All the Scenes While They’re in Class’
description: Adriano Goldman, the cinematographer for HBO’s “Harry Potter” series, said in a recent interview with Andre Pilli that the filming conditions for th
body:
Adriano Goldman, the cinematographer for HBO’s “Harry Potter” series, said in a recent interview with Andre Pilli that the filming conditions for the show have been “brutal” because the camera team must work around the young cast’s strict shooting schedules.

	“You have to shoot really fast, and the faster you go, the more you compromise quality,” Goldman said. “There’s no way around it, right? Obviously, we have a lot of resources, but it’s brutal. The kids’ screen time is really brutal. So the methodology is very specific. You practically have to pre-shoot all the scenes while they’re in class.”
	

	Goldman explained that Dominic McLaughlin (Harry Potter), Alastair Stout (Ron Weasley) and Arabella Stanton (Arabella Stanton) come to set for about “an hour and a half,” and the schedule demands he shoots “six setups” before they have to leave again.


	“It’s really hard; you shoot everything out of order, right, because of that kind of scheduling,” Goldman said. “So the process is really complicated, but I’ve done one now.”

	As noted by Goldman, part of the reason the timelines are so strict is that the child stars have to balance on-set schooling with filming. The BBC reported in July 2025 that Warner Bros. Studios Leavesden, the production home for HBO‘s “Harry Potter,” built a temporary school for the show’s many young actors.

	The Three Rivers District Council permitted the studio to “use a series of portable buildings as a school facility for the next decade. The proposed classrooms have been designed to be used by up to 600 pupils during peak periods, when large crowd scenes are shot, but will typically serve about 150 students. It will operate on weekdays between 5:30 am and 8:30 pm so young actors can fit in their actual studies between night shoots, reshoots and location filming.”
	

	The “Harry Potter” series will debut on HBO and HBO Max on December 25, 2026.

schema.headline: HBO’s ‘Harry Potter’ Cinematographer Says It’s ‘Brutal’ Working Around the Young Stars’ Strict Shooting Schedules: ‘Have to Pre-Shoot All the Scenes While They’re in Class’

title: Future Debuts at No. 1 With ‘The Real Me,’ as Rolling Stones and ‘Heated Rivalry’ Soundtrack Also Bow in Top 10
description: We have seen the future of who’s moving up in the list of artists contending to have the most No. 1 albums ever, and its name is Future. The rapper
body:
We have seen the future of who’s moving up in the list of artists contending to have the most No. 1 albums ever, and its name is Future. The rapper debuted on top of the Billboard 200 with “The Real Me,” landing his 12th No. 1 on the chart. That means he’s now in fifth place, by himself, in the ranking of all-time chart-topping album artists.

	“The Real Me” debuted with 131,000 equivalent album units to easily slip in ahead of Olivia Rodrigo’s “You Look Pretty Sad for a Girl So in Love,” which moved another 96,000 units to hold on at No. 2 in its fifth week on the chart.
	

	Previously, on the list of artists with the most No. 1 albums, Future had been tied with Eminem, with 11. With this 12th No. 1, he leaves Eminem behind to hold fifth place all by himself. The four artists ahead of him on that esteemed list are the Beatles (with 19 No. 1s), Drake and Taylor Swift (tied for second with 15 each) and Jay-Z (with 14).


	Two albums besides Future’s debuted in the top 10. The Rolling Stones‘ “Foreign Tongues” bowed at No. 7 with 53,000 equivalent album units. That’s roughly in the same ballpark as a recent album by the Stones’ contemporary, Paul McCartney, which debuted with 63,000 in June.

	The remaining top 10 entry was the soundtrack for television’s “Heated Rivalry,” which premiered in the No. 9 slot with 41,000 units. Billboard reported that this marked the first time a TV soundtrack had bowed in the top 10 in more than a decade — specifically, since the “Empire” soundtrack did it in 2015. (One other TV soundtrack, “Hazbin Hotel,” the companion album for an animated show, did reach the top 10 last year, but only after debuting at No. 70.)
	

	Future’s figures for “The Real Me,” broken down, show streaming numbers far outstripping sales, which is typical for hip-hop. The album sold only 13,000 copies, but had 120.29 million on-demand streams, which translated into 118,000 SEA units.

	Of course, the Stones’ numbers skewed the opposite way, with 48,500 albums sold, augmented by 4.48 million on-demand streams, amounting to 4,000 SEA units.

	Curiously, the “Heated Rivalry” numbers came down entirely to sales, with 41,000 copies sold on CD and vinyl and 41,000 units altogether. That’s because the score album already came out digitally back in January, but it never accrued enough units on streaming alone to impact the chart prior to the July physical release.

	Looking at the top 10, one big name is MIA: Madonna’s “Confessions II” has dropped out, after debuting last week at No. 1. Its chart position in week 2 isn’t yet known, as Luminate and Billboard only release the list of the top 10 albums on Sunday, so news of exactly where “Confessions II” came in in its sophomore session will have to wait till Monday.

	The rest of the top 10 is rounded out by Morgan Wallen’s “I’m the Problem” at No. 3, Ella Langley at No. 4, Noah Kahan at No. 5, Drake at No. 6, Michael Jackson’s “Thriller” at No. 8 and Wallen’s “One Thing at a Time” at No. 10.

schema.headline: Future Debuts at No. 1 With ‘The Real Me,’ as Rolling Stones and ‘Heated Rivalry’ Soundtrack Also Bow in Top 10

title: Tom Cruise and Half of Hollywood Light Up 2026 World Cup Final While Trump Is Booed
description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup has smashed ratings records in the U.S. and around the world, owing to its unique mix of star power — Lionel Messi, Cristian
body:
The 2026 FIFA World Cup has smashed ratings records in the U.S. and around the world, owing to its unique mix of star power — Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo and Neymar performing in perhaps their final World Cup matches, joined by younger stars Kylian Mbappe, Erling Haaland and Lamine Yamal — a North American setting and a bevy of certified studs. And speaking of star power, Tom Cruise, one of Hollywood’s last movie stars, has an Oscar-courting movie coming out in the fall, “Digger,” that he’s been promoting the heck out of. So naturally, the two would merge their audiences, first with “Digger” airing a number of special TV spots during World Cup matches, and then Cruise making a special appearance during Sunday’s World Cup final between Spain and defending champions Argentina at New Jersey’s MetLife Stadium (temporarily renamed the New York New Jersey Stadium just for the World Cup).
	


	After hanging with “Sesame Street’s” Elmo and Cookie Monster prior to the match, the “Mission: Impossible” and “Top Gun” star delivered an inspirational speech opening the World Cup final, where he proclaimed, “So, as we gather for one final chapter, let us celebrate a tournament that brought the world together. Let us celebrate each other. This is football. This is unity. This is greatness.” A number of other celebrities were in attendance at the final, including President Donald Trump (who was showered with boos) and first lady Melania, Beyoncé and Jay-Z, “The Odyssey” star Matt Damon and his wife Luciana, Timothée Chalamet and his girlfriend Kylie Jenner, David and Victoria Beckham, Serena Williams, Mick Jagger, Dua Lipa, Olivia Rodrigo, Javier Bardem and Penélope Cruz, the Muppets, Will Ferrell, Tom Brady, Kevin Hart, Martha Stewart, Victor Wembanyama, Post Malone, Pharrell Williams (who announcers misidentified as A$AP Rocky), Julia Garner, Trevor Noah, Anya Taylor-Joy (sporting an Argentina scarf, since she’s part-Argentinian), two-time Oscar winner Adrien Brody and more.
	

	And then came the halftime show.

Tom Cruise gives an inspirational speech while opening the FIFA #WorldCup final: "So as we gather for one final chapter, let us celebrate a tournament that brought the world together. Let us celebrate each other. This is football. This is unity. This is greatness."(via FOX) pic.twitter.com/xNxQPhnaqA— Variety (@Variety) July 19, 2026


	Curated by Coldplay’s Chris Martin, the World Cup’s first-ever official halftime show was co-headlined by BTS, Justin Bieber, Madonna and Shakira, highlighting the competition’s mostly American backdrop and its international scope. With the match scoreless at the half, the crowds in attendance and watching on TV needed an adrenaline boost, and the medley of musical acts was more than eager to provide one.

	Oh, and there was Jason Sudeikis, who popped out as his Ted Lasso character to introduce Justin Bieber, urging him to “make us proud” while posing in front of a “BELIEBE” sign.


	To say that the event was star-studded would be a vast understatement. The 2026 FIFA World Cup final was the hottest ticket since Game 4 of the NBA Finals, attracting celebrities from all over the world to… New Jersey.

schema.headline: Tom Cruise and Half of Hollywood Light Up 2026 World Cup Final While Trump Is Booed

title: Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber and Shakira Join Forces for Blink-and-You’ll-Miss-‘Em Moments in a Cluster-FIFA of a Halftime Show
description: The FIFA World Cup Final Halftime Show provided an answer to the burning question of how you fit Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber, Shakira, Burna Boy, Gu
body:
The FIFA World Cup Final Halftime Show provided an answer to the burning question of how you fit Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber, Shakira, Burna Boy, Gustavo Dudamel, Chris Martin and the Muppets into a single 11-minute segment: as fleetingly as possible.

	Although there were reports that the halftime extravaganza would be allowed to go over the allotted 11 minutes, it indeed came in at just a few seconds over that mark, which meant that none of the superstars involved got more than about two minutes of time on the field… or mostly off it, in the case of Madonna.
	

	The segment opened with an apparently pre-taped segment in which Madonna and passengers Ronaldinho and Ronaldo Nazário were carried along on a jeep through tunnels, apparently in the bowels of New York New Jersey Stadium — a journey that lasted long enough that it seemed in doubt whether she would ever make an appearance out on the field. She did, but by that time, her performance of “Music” was over, with nothing left to do in sight of the live fans but strike a pose before the action moved on to Dudamel and the Muppets.


	The transition at the two-minute mark found the outgoing LA Phil conductor leading string sections from New York and Venezuela in supporting a Muppet band’s rendition of the White Stripes’ “Seven Nation Army.” The next minute was given over to leaving viewers wondering why puppets rather than Jack White himself were squeezed into the mega-medley to deliver the biggest riff in rock or sportsdom, before BTS took over at the three-minute point.



	

					RM, Jin, Suga, J-Hope, Jimin, V and Jung Kook of BTS perform during the Topps Final Halftime Show in the FIFA World Cup 2026 Final match in East Rutherford, New Jersey. 
									Getty Images
	



	The K-pop superstars got in a minute and 40 seconds’ worth of “Dynamite,” complete with some altered football lyrics, before they were succeeded by an unbilled appearance by Jason Sudeikis, briefly reprising his role as Ted Lasso. His entree into the World Cup: coaching Justin Bieber, just he went onto the field with an acoustic guitar to sing a fully solo rendition of “Everything Hallelujah,” from his “Swag II” album, sans a band (or laptop).
	



	

					Justin Bieber performs during the Topps Final Halftime Show the FIFA World Cup 2026 Final match in East Rutherford, New Jersey. 
									Getty Images
	





	

					Shakira performs during the Topps Final Halftime Show the FIFA World Cup 2026 Final match in East Rutherford, New Jersey. 
									Getty Images
	



	Who would be “headliner” in this lineup? Not surprisingly, it was Shakira, joined by Burna Boy, singing “Dai Dai,” the FIFA anthem that has “Here in this place you belong” as its brotherhood-of-men-and-feet sentiment. Then, with a minute and a half to go, it was back to the Muppets, now joined by the Webby Award-winning PS22 Chorus and one much taller fellow in the chorus line, Chris Martin, who’d curated the halftime. “We believe in love,” they sang, a sentiment that came in second here only to a belief in brevity, with a more-is-better approach to making the final’s historic first halftime show as much of a cluster-FIFA as possible.

	Earlier in the day, there was no hurry at all when Post Malone got to sing not just one but three numbers during the opening ceremony — including a partial version of “Chrome Heartbreaker,” teasing his upcoming, long-delayed “Eternal Buzz” album. Also getting an unusual amount of time, at least compared to the halftime superstars, was streamer/influencer-turned-singer ISHowSpeed, reprising his 2022 World Cup anthem, the prosaically titled “World Cup.” 

	Jennifer Hudson delivered the U.S. national anthem, giving the day its most honest jolt of genuine, un-lip-synched lung power. Robbie Williams, Nicole Scherzinger and Laura Pausini teamed up for another official FIFA theme song, “Desire,” which included Williams singing the line “Love is the law,” a directive that will no doubt hold true unless the president makes a call to get it overturned.



	

					Nicole Scherzinger, Robbie Williams and Laura Pausini perform before the FIFA World Cup 2026 Final match between Spain and Argentina at New York New Jersey Stadium on July 19, 2026 in East Rutherford, New Jersey. 
									Getty Images
	





	

					Post Malone performs during the closing ceremony before the FIFA World Cup 2026 Final match in East Rutherford, New Jersey. 
									Getty Images
	





	

					Jennifer Hudson performs the U.S. national anthem before the FIFA World Cup 2026 Final match in East Rutherford, New Jersey. 
									Getty Images
	





	

					Chris Martin performs during the Topps Final Halftime Show in the FIFA World Cup 2026 Final match  in East Rutherford, New Jersey. 
									Getty Images

schema.headline: Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber and Shakira Join Forces for Blink-and-You’ll-Miss-‘Em Moments in a Cluster-FIFA of a Halftime Show

title: ‘House of the Dragon’ Star Fabien Frankel on Criston Cole’s Suicide Mission and His ‘Nihilism’: ‘He’s Losing the Will to Motivate’
description:
SPOILER ALERT: This article contains spoilers for Season 3, Episode 5 of “House of the Dragon,” now streaming on HBO Max.

	In tonight’s “House of t
body:
SPOILER ALERT: This article contains spoilers for Season 3, Episode 5 of “House of the Dragon,” now streaming on HBO Max.

	In tonight’s “House of the Dragon” episode, Ser Criston Cole (Fabien Frankel) proclaims that he’s ready to die — and he’s ready to do so honorably.

	As the war to capture the Iron Throne rages on, Criston marches onward with his men, including Gwayne Hightower (Freddie Fox), to put a stop to the Rivermen reaching King’s Landing and aiding Rhaenyra Targaryen (Emma D’Arcy). But their journey to catch up with the Rivermen proves increasingly difficult, after one of their horses gets caught in a trap. Once they come across a burned-down bridge to cross the river, they realize there is no chance of catching up.
	

	Gwayne and Criston regroup and debate what to do next, now down to half the men they started with. Gwayne says continuing forward “would march us toward certain death.” That’s when Gwayne realizes this was Criston’s goal all along, as Criston launches into a revealing monologue: “I have been a servant all my life. To Green or to Black. I have defined myself by oaths, through unyielding devotion. And the highborn have made much of me while it pleased them to do so. But I will not let them diminish me now. I will not return spurned, rejected. I am the realm’s greatest warrior. And I will die as such.”


	While Frankel says he doesn’t read fan reactions much these days, it has become easier to tap into his controversial character’s motivations in Season 3: “I can only understand why people feel how they feel about him as a character. It’s tricky for me as an actor, because obviously, I just don’t see him in that way. It serves no purpose for me to see him in that way. You always have to be the bastion of your character, and I feel very much the bastion of Criston Cole.”
	

	Frankel spoke with Variety to break down Criston’s suicide mission, why being a soldier is “the only honest version of himself,” and what fans can expect — should the series continue to follow George R.R. Martin’s book “A Song of Ice and Fire,” as expected — from the upcoming Butcher’s Ball.
	
		This is a pivotal moment for Criston, proclaiming that he is ready to die an honorable death. Can you talk about his journey throughout this episode, and how he arrives at this point?	

	It’s something that’s been brewing inside of him so much over the course of the show, probably since about halfway through Season 2. These thoughts of, “What am I going to be, and what’s the legacy I’m going to leave behind? In what way do I want to go out?” I suppose that really comes to a head in that fire scene with Gwayne.
	
		Does he have a specific vision for how he wants his death to play out? How can he guarantee he’s going to go out the way he wants, and that people will remember him?	

	I don’t know if there’s anything in his head — it’s like he said, “Hell bent in glory,” right? Criston Cole started as a soldier before the 20-year stretch that he spent at King’s Landing — that’s how he started, and that’s how he wants to finish. In his mind, the only honest version of himself is the soldier. Everything else is pretense; the diplomat is pretense; the Hand of the King, there’s a level of pretense; Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, there’s a level of pretense. I suppose they’re all psychological, brain-led jobs. He isn’t that way, he’s very much led by his sword and his fighting instinct. The idea that that’s how he will be remembered, I think, feels honest.
	
		Gwayne eventually figures out what’s really going on. Criston is very upfront about his suicide mission once it clicks.	

	There’s a genuine fondness that Criston has for Gwayne; there’s a yin-yang aspect to them. Weirdly, I think Gwayne represents — on the surface — everything that Criston disdains in this world. Yet underneath, Criston’s found someone who will go to battle with him, who does have a heart, who does have empathy, who is much more than he appears. So their relationship is built through this, these guys going to war with each other. You hear any soldier talk about their time in the war, in any war, and they’ll say the relationships they formed are closer than anything you can possibly imagine because you’re living through life and death with these people. You’re risking your lives for each other. As a result of that, there’s a profoundness to their relationship.
	



	

									Theo Whiteman/HBO
	


	
		Why does Criston, then, decide not to tell Gwayne about his suicide mission for this long?	

	I don’t think he’s waiting for Gwayne to figure it out, it’s not a game. Certainly, I didn’t think of it as a game. I think he just knew that as soon as he told Gwayne, that would create a lack of desire to continue. Gwayne’s dislike of this plan would rub off on the rest of the men, and then Criston would lose an army that otherwise would have a good chance of fighting. There’s undeniably a selfishness to Criston’s plan, right? He’s looking out for himself but I think he’s become so accustomed to a world where every single person is looking out for themselves constantly. Unfortunately, any sort of attempt at selflessness falls on itself. For any of the characters, it’s just a very dog-eat-dog world.
	
		You’ve spoken in previous interviews about not always knowing Criston’s code, or where his motivations lie episode to episode. As the season has continued on, have you been able to tap into these more?	

	There’s a freedom that comes with nihilism, to a degree. This is a character who, at least at the top of Season 2 and toward the end of Season 3, surrendered himself to the notion that the war is over. That ultimately, we as human beings have no genuinely important part to play in a war that’s ultimately going to be won by dragons or lost by dragons. They are just so much more powerful, and so you meet him at the top of Season 3 in a genuinely kind of — I wouldn’t say nihilistic, because I think that’s a bit reductive. But certainly, there’s an agnosticism to him. He’s losing the will to motivate. He finds that will as the course of the season goes on, and certainly in Episode 4’s planning scene with Gwayne that really gives him the impetus. It re-energizes him and then he certainly has rediscovered his sense of code — not code so much, but a sense of purpose.
	
		This episode comes on the heels of Criston finding out that Aemond (Ewan Mitchell) has left him behind, and he’s once again hit with those feelings of abandonment that he’s felt all his life. How does that play into his actions here?	

	It’s tricky, man. I want to think there’s a part of him that is intelligent enough — not intelligent enough — but a part of him that certainly recognizes who Aemond is. I just don’t believe it’s all that surprising that Aemond hasn’t shown, even if he’s playing. Sometimes in order to sell a story as people, we go along with notions that feel like the most natural ones. So Criston saying, “He wouldn’t desert us, I can’t believe that,” I almost feel like that’s him playing into the version of this character that needs that piece of information in order to rediscover a sense of self and purpose.
	
	
		How does he look back on his relationships with Rhaenyra and Alicent Hightower (Olivia Cooke) now — does he have regrets? As he’s spending these long periods traveling to battle, ready to fall on his sword, is he reflecting on his decisions, or is he just focused on charging forward?	

	Haven’t men always done that in history? Just march on, and go to work, “I’m not going to deal with anything that I’m really feeling, I’m just going to go on and get on and do something, and that thing is going to supersede whatever I’m feeling!” But it’s inevitable that Rhaenyra and Alicent are both on his mind all the time. I think Alicent much more than Rhaenyra. Certainly for me, Rhaenyra has become a sort of afterthought. And actually, it’s just the idea of winning the war for Alicent.
	
		Has it become easier to tap into Criston’s humanity, despite him being such a controversial character?	

	There’s just genuine insight into who this man is, and what makes him tick. Yeah, I think I have found it easier to tap into the humanity of him. It’s difficult, you can’t undo his actions. He’s done the things that he’s done, and those things have been, at times, very morally reprehensible or questionable, to say the least. And so I can only understand why people feel how they feel about him as a character. It’s tricky for me as an actor because obviously, I just don’t see him in that way. It serves no purpose for me to see him in that way. You always have to be the bastion of your character, and I feel very much the bastion of Criston Cole.
	
		How do you look back on your journey on the show and playing Criston?	

	I learned so much from playing Criston Cole. There’s a stillness to him that I don’t naturally have as a person, and I think I’ve learned so much through that. We’re so different as people, but I look back on the last few years with just a ton of warmth towards the people I’ve gotten to work with. Irrespective of playing the character or making the show, it’s the company that you keep that makes any experience.
	
		Your former “House of the Dragon” co-star, Milly Alcock, is having a big summer with “Supergirl.” Have you had a chance to see the film, do you keep in touch with Milly at all?	

	I keep in touch with her all the time, yeah! I spoke to her on the phone two weeks ago when she called me from Brazil — I think she was doing press. I’m immensely proud of her, and not in the least bit surprised. I told my agent he should sign her as soon as we did Season 1 of “House of the Dragon” together, and that’s what he did. I’m so happy for her, very proud of her. I haven’t yet had a chance to see “Supergirl,” but I will!
	
	
		I know fans of the book will be looking forward to Butcher’s Ball, which the end of this week’s episode seems to set up. What can you say about the action scenes coming up?	

	All I can say is that it was long and arduous choreography to learn, it’s a brilliantly choreographed fight. There are a ton of unbelievable stuntmen in it that make the fight what it is, and I’m just super excited for people to see that side of Criston. It’s the side of him we haven’t really seen at all — what he is like in a man-to-man situation.

	This interview has been edited and condensed.

schema.headline: ‘House of the Dragon’ Star Fabien Frankel on Criston Cole’s Suicide Mission and His ‘Nihilism’: ‘He’s Losing the Will to Motivate’

title: J.D. Vance and Wife Usha Welcome Baby Boy, Make Vice Presidential History
description:
J.D. And Usha Vance
                          Welcome to the Family, Baby Alec!!!
body:
J.D. And Usha Vance
                          Welcome to the Family, Baby Alec!!!
                      
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Vice President J.D. Vance and Second Lady Usha Vance just added another little Vance to the family ... welcoming their fourth child and making a little history in the process.
      
    
            The couple announced Sunday their baby boy, Alec Neel Vance, was born earlier in the morning ... saying both mom and Alec are happy and healthy, while their three older children are "overjoyed" to meet their new brother.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            The birth is also one for the history books -- Alec is reportedly the first child born to a sitting U.S. vice president in more than 150 years. The last was Schuyler Colfax III, whose father was serving as VP when he arrived in 1870.
      

            Alec joins siblings Ewan, 9, Vivek, 6, and Mirabel, 4 ... giving the vice president a full house at the Naval Observatory.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            J.D. and Usha thanked the doctors and staff at Walter Reed National Military Medical Center, along with the White House medical team, for caring for their growing family.
      
    
            The new arrival fits right in with J.D.'s outspoken push for Americans to have more kids ... he declared at the 2025 March for Life rally, "I want more babies in the United States of America."
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Usha announced her pregnancy in January and later got plenty of attention for rocking an $8.75 Old Navy maternity dress -- prompting J.D. to jokingly nominate his bargain-hunting wife as the country's next budget director.
      
    
            The VP has wanted more babies in America -- and the Vances are leading by example.
      
      
              
          
                          
                J.D. Vance
              
                          
                Family
              
                          
                White House
              
                          
                Politics
              
                          
                News

schema.headline: J.D. Vance and Wife Usha Welcome Baby Boy, Make Vice Presidential History

title: Lady Gaga Celebrates Manager Bobby Campbell's Wedding With Fiancé
description:
Lady Gaga & Michael Polansky
                          Date Night for Her Manager's Big 'I Do'
body:
Lady Gaga & Michael Polansky
                          Date Night for Her Manager's Big 'I Do'
                      
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Lady Gaga was in celebration mode this weekend ... 'cause TMZ has learned she and her fiancé, Michael Polansky, stepped out for date night at the wedding of her longtime manager and close friend, Bobby Campbell, and his husband.
      
    
            Sources with direct knowledge tell TMZ ... Gaga and Polansky attended the Saturday night ceremony at the Walt Disney Concert Hall in downtown Los Angeles, where Campbell and Robert García tied the knot surrounded by family and friends.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            We're told Gaga kept a low profile throughout the evening, blending in with the rest of the guests instead of stealing the spotlight. And despite her superstar status, our sources say she did not perform during the reception.
      

            Instead, we're told "Gypsy Woman" singer Crystal Waters provided the night's entertainment, getting guests on their feet during the celebration.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Campbell and Gaga go way back. He's managed the pop icon for years and has helped steer some of the biggest moments of her career, from chart-topping albums and global tours to her Oscar-winning era and recent projects.
      
    
            Their relationship has long extended beyond business, with Gaga frequently praising Campbell publicly and the two sharing a close friendship.
      
      
              
          
                          
                Michael Polansky
              
                          
                Lady Gaga

schema.headline: Lady Gaga Celebrates Manager Bobby Campbell's Wedding With Fiancé

title: Anne Hathaway Then & Now -- Good Genes or Good Docs, Hot Shots
description:
Anne Hathaway Then & Now
                          Good Genes or Good Docs?!
body:
Anne Hathaway Then & Now
                          Good Genes or Good Docs?!
                          Hot Shots 🔥
                      
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Anne Hathaway's looks over the years are "Prada"-approved from head to toe!
      
    
            Here is an 18-year-old version of the young Hollywood actress showing off her bedazzled décolletage at "The Princess Diaries" Premiere back in 2001 (left).
      
    
            And, 25 years later, Anne was glowing at the "The Odyssey" photocall earlier this month in Paris (right).
      
    
            We know she serves elevated style and gives us just enough of that killer silhouette -- and leaves us wanting more -- but the question here is:
      
    
            Good Genes or Good Docs?!

title: Jordan Clarkson, Travis Scott Removed From NYC Club After Confrontation
description:
Travis Scott & Jordan Clarkson
                          Booted from Club After Bottles Fly!!!
body:
Travis Scott & Jordan Clarkson
                          Booted from Club After Bottles Fly!!!
                      
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            A star-studded night at Zero Bond took a chaotic turn Saturday ... ending with NBA champion Jordan Clarkson being escorted out by police.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            A source familiar with the situation tells TMZ ... a man was allegedly filming Travis Scott chatting with another attendee inside the NYC hot spot when Travis allegedly confronted him.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            We’re told members of Travis' entourage then allegedly began throwing bottles ... and Jordan jumped in to de-escalate the situation. Both were ultimately removed from the venue.
      

            Jordan, of course, recently helped the New York Knicks capture their first NBA championship in 53 years ... but Saturday night, his defense apparently extended beyond the basketball court.
      
    
            As TMZ previously reported ... Zero Bond was packed with celebrities for the Raising Cane's bash, including Tate McRae, Alix Earle, Jake and Logan Paul, and plenty more.
      
    
        Waiting for your permission to load the Instagram Media.
      
    
            50 Cent also took the stage during the star-studded bash ... performing for the packed crowd before the night took its chaotic turn.
      
    
            We’ve reached out to reps for Travis, Jordan, Zero Bond and the NYPD ... so far, no word back.
